text.title: caching-http-headers Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for caching-http-headers as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Setting Vary: * on public API responses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vary: * tells CDN nodes the response cannot be cached at all (every request is unique). This is correct for truly request-specific responses but is routinely misapplied to public endpoints, eliminating all CDN caching benefit.

failure-mode.description: Using Cache-Control: public on responses that contain user-specific data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A shared CDN cache will store the response and serve it to subsequent users. One user's profile, orders, or session data becomes visible to other users matching the same cache key. This is a security vulnerability, not just a caching mistake.
